title: Uniface XML Stream Processing
text: Uniface XML Stream Processing Uniface uses the xmlsave and xmlload ProcScript statements to transfer and convert data (including validation and modification state) between Uniface component and XML streams. Attributes determine the state of data in an XML stream. These are defined in the DTD for the XML stream. Only fields of the type database or non-database are allowed in XML streams. The value of a static field or control field cannot be included or loaded into an XML stream when using xmlsave and xmlload . Writing XML Data The xmlsave statement saves the data from fields (and their modification and validation state) on a component into an XML stream, converting it to comply with the XML 1.0 standard. The XML stream is stored in a variable or parameter with xmlstream data type. xmlsave does not change the original field values on a component, fire validation triggers, or change the active path. String data in each field is converted to comply with the XML 1.0 standard. This includes replacing angle brackets (< and >), ampersand (&), and quotation marks (' and ") with appropriate escape sequences. In the XML 1.0 standard, these escape sequences are called 'predefined entities'. Raw and image data types are converted into strings using a base 64 encoding. Numeric data is converted to string data, using the display format of the field. If a DTD specifies meta information attributes for an element, then meta information about the modification and validation state is included in the XML stream for that element. For entity elements, modification and validation state can be included. For field elements, only validation state can be included in the XML stream. Reading XML Data The xmlload statement extracts data from an XML stream and places the data as disconnected occurrences on the component. It uses a DTD and a element-to-field map to do this conversion. Element values are converted into Uniface field values. xmlload also copies the state specified in the attributes to occurrences loaded from the stream. If Uniface encounters data validation error messages in an XML stream, it fires the appropriate error trigger for each error. Uniface uses attributes to determine the state of data in an XML stream. If these attributes are specified in the DTD and are encountered in the XML stream, the state specified in the attributes is copied to occurrences loaded from the stream, and is applied during reconnect by retrieve/reconnect . Data validation error messages can be present in the XML stream. If Uniface encounters these, the error trigger is fired for each validation error included in the stream. For example, the error trigger could change the display color of a field to indicate a validation error. Data Type Conversion Table 1. Conversion of Uniface data types to PCDATA in XML streams Uniface data type Converted to... Description String String, with substitution of predefined entities No conversion takes place, except: Substitution of predefined entities for quote ("), apostrophe (’), angle bracket (< and >) and ampersand (&) characters. The form feed character #xC is converted to //f in XML streams Profile characters and subfield separators are converted to a backslash (/) keystroke combination. For example, GOLD * is converted to /*. (This is not applicable if the xmlload/noprofile switch is specified.) Backslash is converted to double backslash (//), because backslash itself is a meta character used in regular expressions. Raw data String, with substitution of predefined entities The field is converted into a string using an internal base 64 encoding. Numeric String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Floating decimal point String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Date String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Time String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Combined date and time String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Boolean String "F" (false) or "T" (true). Image (all types) String, with substitution of predefined entities The field is converted into a string using an internal base 64 encoding. Linear date String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Linear time String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger. Linear date and time String, with substitution of predefined entities The format of the string is based on the field’s Display format, or on the format specified in the formatToDisplay trigger.
